claim_text: Soldier and 'Silent' Vote Are Believed to Give Slight Advantage to Roosevelt in New Jersey
Morton says Nixon is slightly behind in Missouri
Ridder sees a victory and tells Bryan that the German vote will be for him.
Vermont and Maine conceded to Hoover; Democrats due to win in Rhode Island
Republican victory indicated in Maine; some predict 50,000 plurality in state election tomorrow
Vice President Al Gore has edged ahead of Gov. George W. Bush in the battle for electoral votes, according to independent and partisan analysts.
Democrats expected to win at least one House seat
Indicates Hughes's Election.
Race in Illinois called toss-up; Nixon appears to be ahead but the Democrats may take two top posts
Dewey is favored to win in Virginia, but the edge is so narrow a mild shift in 'silent votes' would defeat him.
Senator Ladd insists that 'Fighting Bob' La Follette will sweep the Northwest in the upcoming election.
In battleground states, like Missouri and Ohio, he is running strongly
Southwest for Wilson; McLemore says Mexican policy has made only New Mexico doubtful.
Ex-Alabama Governor Wallace thought to lead in 9 of 11 states of Old Confederacy, with total of 91 electoral votes
Definite swing is to Hoover in Minnesota, but result is in doubt.
Hoover's majority in Ohio is estimated by political observers from 250,000 to 650,000.
Times (Wehrwein) survey of Missouri finds Kennedy leads; Democrats report gains in rural area
Senator Burrows confident; says Republicans will carry Michigan
Wallace and Nixon in close competition for conservative vote in South; Wallace leading in all but 2 of 11 Southern states
Crucial Midwestern states are seen tipping markedly, if not yet decisively, in Pres Clinton's direction
Dewey and Wiley are reported having a slight edge in Wisconsin
200,000 Roosevelt plurality predicted in Ohio
Coos County, NH, voters indicate they will support Johnson against Goldwater in the upcoming presidential election.
